mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, compressing required returns and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent, or the signal loses statistical significance after controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand and consumer confidence, generating sustained positive equity returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or 60-day forward returns are negative in over 60 percent of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during late-cycle expansions reflects capacity utilization nearing peaks and corporate earnings acceleration, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during late-cycle phases, or 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of rising INDPRO observations within this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 0.5 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, raising discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite high inflation prints, or 20-day forward returns are positive in over 65 percent of threshold-crossing events.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment confirms a strengthening labor market, boosting corporate revenue forecasts and driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses predictive power when tested against a univariate PAYEMS model, or 60-day returns are negative in more than 55 percent of joint-signal activations.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth surpassing 2.0 percent confirms sustained economic expansion, validating corporate earnings trajectories and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: GDP prints above 2.0 percent fail to precede positive equity returns, or the signal produces negative 60-day returns in over 60 percent of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with stagnant or rising policy rates creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and equity valuations, driving negative monthly returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets remain resilient or rally during high-inflation, high-rate periods, or 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of stagflationary observations.
